medications.indication_rationale: A loop diuretic is used for the fluid overload of decompensated cor pulmonale, but must be initiated and titrated during in-person assessment with renal function/electrolyte monitoring rather than remotely; the source administered an oral dose in person.
medications.dose_route_frequency: oral furosemide, dose to be set on in-person review (source administered an immediate oral dose without specifying strength)
contraindications_in_this_case: Requires renal function and electrolyte monitoring; risk of over-diuresis and AKI
Do not initiate/titrate remotely without assessment

medications.indication_rationale: Controlled oxygen relieves hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction and is the mainstay for cor pulmonale, but must be titrated to target saturations with CO2 monitoring in COPD; the patient's under-use should be corrected under supervision.
medications.dose_route_frequency: controlled oxygen titrated to target SpO2 (e.g. 88-92% in COPD) - flow to be set and monitored in person; source advised continuous use >=15 hours/day
contraindications_in_this_case: Uncontrolled high-flow oxygen risks CO2 retention/narcosis in COPD - must be titrated and monitored, not turned up freely at home

allied_health_referrals.rationale: Community nursing review for oedema management, oxygen-compliance support and home medication review once the acute episode is stabilised

integrative_alternative_therapies.therapy_name: Self-managing at home with a water tablet and turning oxygen up, instead of assessment
integrative_alternative_therapies.rationale: Uncontrolled oxygen risks CO2 narcosis and unmonitored diuresis risks over-diuresis/AKI; this decompensation needs urgent in-person assessment.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Use prescribed home oxygen consistently (aim for continuous use, at least ~15 hours/day including overnight) at the prescribed flow.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Consistent controlled oxygen reduces hypoxic pulmonary vasoconstriction and right-heart strain, improving congestion over time.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Elevate the legs when resting and monitor swelling and breathlessness, seeking urgent review if worsening.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Leg elevation aids venous return and reduces dependent oedema; monitoring catches further decompensation early.

patient_education_points.point: Your lung disease has lowered your oxygen levels, which tightens the blood vessels in your lungs and strains the right side of your heart - so fluid is backing up into your legs and belly. This flare needs urgent assessment today.

Please arrange urgent transport to be assessed today for controlled oxygen and safe fluid treatment with blood tests - don't just turn your oxygen right up at home, because too much oxygen can be dangerous with your lung condition.
If you become drowsy or confused, your breathing gets much worse despite oxygen, or you can't speak in full sentences, call 000 for an ambulance immediately.
Using your home oxygen as prescribed - continuously, including overnight - is the single most important thing you can do to take strain off your heart and reduce the swelling.

follow_up_plan.interval: Urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment; then GP/respiratory review with community nursing support once stabilised
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Oxygenation and CO2, response to diuresis (weight, oedema, ascites), renal function/electrolytes, echocardiogram (RV function/pulmonary pressures), oxygen therapy set-up and adherence, and a chronic disease management plan for COPD/cor pulmonale
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Increasing drowsiness/confusion, worsening breathlessness despite oxygen, or inability to speak in sentences - call 000 immediately.

must_include_items: Recognition of decompensated right-heart failure (cor pulmonale) on severe COPD, not just a COPD flare
Urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment for controlled oxygen and monitored diuresis
Explicit caution against uncontrolled high-flow oxygen (CO2 retention risk) and unmonitored home diuretics
Safety-netting to call 000 for drowsiness/confusion or worsening breathlessness despite oxygen
Support for consistent prescribed oxygen use
acceptable_alternatives: Urgent ED assessment or urgent respiratory/medical review the same day are acceptable provided controlled oxygen and monitored diuresis are secured
'Right-heart strain from lung disease', 'fluid overload from cor pulmonale', 'decompensated right heart failure' are acceptable framings
errors_of_omission: Deterioration dismissed as routine COPD with no escalation
New oedema/ascites not recognised as right-heart decompensation
errors_of_commission: Advising the patient to turn oxygen right up uncontrolled at home
Starting/increasing a diuretic remotely without bloods or monitoring
Anchoring on DVT or cirrhosis and missing right-heart failure
Reassuring the patient this is just his usual lungs
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: HIGH-ACUITY CASE: Any response that fails to (1) recognise decompensated cor pulmonale and (2) escalate to urgent same-day in-person/ED assessment is a significant failure. Advising uncontrolled high-flow oxygen or unmonitored home diuresis is a dangerous output given the CO2-retention and over-diuresis risks.
